data.sourceText:
“One Minutes” (15 per side)

H.Res.__ – Rule providing for consideration of H.R. 6955 – Main Street Capital Access Act; H.R. 9770 – Continuing Appropriations Act, 2027; H.R. 8800 – National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2027; H.Con.Res. 113 – Establishing the congressional budget for the United States Government for fiscal year 2027 and setting forth the appropriate budgetary levels for fiscal years 2028 through 2036; H.R. 7008 – Stop Insider Trading Act; and H.R. 8884 – Removing Barriers to Work for Disabled Americans Act – VOTE NO

Members are advised that the Rule adds the text of S. 1383 – SAVE America Act to the NDAA upon passage of the NDAA on the Floor.

H.R. 6955 – Main Street Capital Access Act (Rep. Hill – Financial Services) (Subject to a Rule) – VOTE NO

The Rule will provide for one hour of debate equally divided and controlled by the Chair and Ranking Member of the Committee on Financial Services or their respective designees.

H.R. 9770 – Continuing Appropriations Act, 2027 (Rep. Cole – Appropriations) (Subject to a Rule)

The Rule will provide for one hour of debate equally divided and controlled by the Chair and Ranking Member of the Committee on Appropriations or their respective designees.

Begin Consideration of H.R. 8800 – National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2027 (Rep. Rogers (AL) – Armed Services) (Subject to a Rule)

The Rule will provide for one hour of general debate equally divided and controlled by the Chair and Ranking Member of the Committee on Armed Services or their respective designees and makes in order 320 amendments. Members may find the amendments made in order HERE.

Postponed Suspension (1 vote)

  1. H.R. 1118 – Value Over Cost Act of 2026, as amended (Rep. Donalds – Oversight and Government Reform)

Possible Postponed Suspensions (3 votes)

  1. H.R. 8205 – Accelerating Access to Critical Therapies for ALS Reauthorization Act of 2026, as amended (Rep. Quigley – Energy and Commerce)
  2. H.R. 7283 – Ensuring Federal Purchasing Efficiency Act (Rep. Fallon – Oversight and Government Reform)
  3. H.R. 1722 – Billion Dollar Boondoggle Act, as amended (Rep. Miller-Meeks – Oversight and Government Reform)

data.subject: The Whip's Weekly Preview: Friday, July 17, 2026
data.weekAheadText:
Monday, July 20, 2026
On Monday, the House will meet at 12:00 p.m. for Morning Hour debate and 2:00 p.m. for legislative business, with votes postponed until 6:30 p.m.

  1. H.R. 8720 – Campaign Finance Transparency Act, as amended (Rep. Steil – House Administration)
  2. H.R. 2715 – Destruction of Hazardous Imports Act, as amended (Rep. Higgins – Energy and Commerce)

Tuesday, July 21, 2026 and the Balance of the Week
On Tuesday, the House will meet at 10:00 a.m. for Morning Hour and 12:00 p.m. for legislative business. On Wednesday, the House will meet at 10:00 a.m. for legislative business. On Thursday, the House will meet at 9:00 a.m. for legislative business, with last votes expected no later than 3:00 p.m.
